title: Perceptions of visual and multimodal symbolic mediated social touch
summary: This paper studies mediated social touch in remote communication and compares visual, haptic, and multimodal symbolic gestures. It argues that touch can operate as a symbolic communication channel whose meaning depends on relationship and emotional context. It is especially interesting because physical haptics are not always necessary for meaningful mediated touch.

related.reason: Both frame touch as communicative and relational rather than merely technical.
Both belong to the mediated social touch cluster.

notes: This paper is important because it partially decouples touch from physical haptics. A projector-camera system without haptics can perform no worse than a haptic one on several measures, which is conceptually very useful.
quotes: Touch is treated here as a symbolic communication channel.
